problem_statement: An arts center's environmental dashboard stopped updating temperature and humidity for one building after a weekend maintenance window. Sensors still publish locally, and dashboards for the other buildings remain current.

observations.statement: The final dashboard sample for building alpha is timestamped 2026-07-04T06:59:58Z; a site overlay rollout completed at 07:00:11Z, and no application or broker deployment occurred that weekend.
observations.provenance: retained dashboard sample index and deployment ledger

observations.statement: Broker-retained messages continue on topics building/alpha/floor1 and building/alpha/floor2 every thirty seconds after the dashboard becomes stale.
The subscriber's effective settings snapshot for alpha contains /building/alpha/#, including a leading slash, while the prior snapshot and the broker topics contain building/alpha/# without one.
observations.provenance: signed effective-settings snapshots and broker inventory

observations.statement: The shared template did not change; the alpha overlay added a path-normalization transform intended for HTTP routes and applied it to the topic filter field during rendering.
observations.provenance: overlay diff and deterministic render trace

observations.statement: Broker subscription logs show the alpha client reconnecting successfully with the leading-slash filter, receiving zero matched messages, and maintaining a healthy connection.
An offline topic-matcher evaluation over the retained broker inventory returns 1,842 matches for building/alpha/# and zero for /building/alpha/#.
observations.provenance: read-only matcher report using broker specification rules

observations.statement: Building beta uses the same application image, broker, sensor models, and shared template but an overlay without the transform; its dashboard remains current, providing a negative control.

The dashboard store has no alpha samples after the rollout and no rejected-write entries, placing the stale display downstream of absent subscriber delivery rather than database rejection or rendering cache behavior.
observations.provenance: database ingestion ledger and write-error archive

observations.statement: Sensor firmware versions and payload schemas are mixed equally across alpha and beta, and retained alpha payloads decode under the current schema, weakening a coincident firmware or payload-format explanation.
observations.provenance: offline payload decode and firmware cohort report
